name: hESC-derived RPE showed graft survival and visual signals in retinal disease
summary: Open-label phase 1/2 studies reported medium-term safety, graft-survival signals, and possible visual benefit after hESC-derived RPE transplantation.
statement: In 18 participants with atrophic age-related macular degeneration or Stargardt macular dystrophy, subretinal hESC-derived RPE transplantation showed no adverse proliferation or rejection signal and was associated with graft-pigmentation and possible visual-acuity or quality-of-life improvement.
population_or_model: People with atrophic age-related macular degeneration or Stargardt macular dystrophy
quantitative_note: The report noted increasing subretinal pigmentation in 13 of 18 treated eyes; best-corrected visual acuity improved in 10 eyes, stayed improved or stable in 7 eyes, and decreased by more than 10 letters in 1 eye.
caveats: The studies were open-label phase 1/2 safety studies without randomized efficacy control.
Adverse events were associated with vitreoretinal surgery and immunosuppression.
AMD and Stargardt retinal replacement evidence is disease-specific and does not demonstrate systemic stem-cell-exhaustion reversal.
